summary: Iteration 243 — Tested 4 UX tweaks: (1) RefundRequestDialog amount read-only, (2) Ratings page compact rows + stats-inside-filter card + preview modal (admin + operator), (3) Customer Service ticket cards (list + grid) smaller white style. Source-level verification: ALL changes are present in the codebase (RefundRequestDialog.jsx has no <Input type=number> for amount and exposes data-testid='refund-amount-readout' + data-testid='refund-amount-value'; Ratings.jsx renders data-testid='admin-rating-row-{id}' / 'operator-rating-row-{id}' compact rows inside a Card whose <CardContent> hosts the stats chip strip below the search Input; CustomerServiceManagement.jsx AdminTicketCard + AdminTicketCardGrid both use bg-white border border-slate-200 p-3 rounded-lg). RUNTIME verification on the deployed preview URL showed a MISMATCH on the Ratings page: admin-ratings-stats testid IS in DOM (count=1) but NO admin-rating-row-* elements render (count=0), and the visible UI still shows the OLD big-card layout with inline Flag/Hide/Delete actions and a stats chip strip OUTSIDE the filter card. Same mismatch for operator view (operator-ratings-stats count=1, operator-rating-row count=0). Customer Service page rendered 0 list and 0 grid ticket cards (page-data may have failed; saw `422` console errors). Refund dialog could not be opened end-to-end — customer@test.com lists 21 orders but none of the openable order entries surfaced a 'Request a refund' button (likely no paid+eligible orders in fixture data). Recommend main agent investigate why the Ratings.jsx changes are not surfacing at runtime — the source has the new JSX but the bundle being served still renders the older big-card branch. Possibility: a second Ratings page/component is being routed (e.g., a wrapper that imports from another file), or a stale build is being served (vite log shows last build was successful, dist/assets/index-BGueu9hI.js).

ui_bugs.issue: Runtime DOM has admin-ratings-stats (count=1) but admin-rating-row-{id} count=0; visible layout is still OLD big cards with inline Flag/Hide/Delete and stats chips ABOVE the filter card, not the new compact rows + stats-inside-card layout from Ratings.jsx lines 1229-1414.
Same as Admin: operator-ratings-stats present (count=1) but operator-rating-row-{id} count=0; old layout visible.

0 ticket-card-* and 0 ticket-card-grid-* elements found in DOM after page load; console shows 422 from a request — could not validate the bg-white/p-3 styling at runtime. Source change itself is correct.

integration_issues.flow: Refund dialog open from customer order
integration_issues.issue: customer@test.com has 21 order entries on /orders but clicking them never surfaced a 'Request a refund' CTA — either no paid+within-window orders OR the trigger button uses a non-standard label/testid. Could not open the dialog to assert refund-amount-readout/value at runtime.

issues: Per screenshot: stats chip strip floats as a separate row above the filter card; per request it should sit INSIDE the filter Card below the search input. Visible behavior mismatches source code on lines 1229-1265.

test_report_links: /app/frontend/src/components/refunds/RefundRequestDialog.jsx (source verified — old refund-amount-input removed, new refund-amount-readout + refund-amount-value present)
action_items: PRIMARY: Investigate why the /ratings page in the running build is still rendering the OLD big-card AdminRatingsView/OperatorRatingsView layout despite Ratings.jsx source containing the new compact rows + preview modal. Confirm there is no duplicate page (e.g., /app/frontend/src/pages/admin/AllRatings.jsx or /app/frontend/src/pages/AdminRatings.jsx) being imported instead — `grep -rn 'AdminRatingsView' /app/frontend/src` returns only Ratings.jsx, so verify the routed page in App.jsx (line 277, path='/ratings') actually mounts Ratings.jsx and not a wrapper that overrides it.

Force a clean rebuild and supervisor restart for the frontend to flush any stale Vite dist bundle (frontend.out.log shows last build dist/assets/index-BGueu9hI.js — confirm Ratings.jsx changes hashed into a new bundle).

Verify /customer-service ticket data loads for admin@test.com — console showed 422 on a fetch which is why 0 ticket cards rendered. Likely the page is calling an endpoint with a missing query param or wrong shape; fix the request so the page populates and the new bg-white/p-3 cards become observable at runtime.

Seed at least one paid, within-window order for customer@test.com so the 'Request a refund' button surfaces from OrderDetailModal and the refund-amount-readout/value can be exercised end-to-end. Source-level the change is correct: no <input type='number'>, requestedAmount state removed, and submit posts eligibility.eligible_amount unchanged.
Optional/leftover from iter242: GET /api/cinema/showtimes/{id}/details still omits refund_policy (out of scope this iteration).
critical_code_review_comments: RefundRequestDialog.jsx: change correctly applied — no requestedAmount useState, no Input for amount, submit body sends `requested_amount: eligibility?.eligible_amount ?? null` which the server overwrites with the policy-determined value. Read-only readout uses tone-coloured surface (emerald/rose).

Ratings.jsx AdminRatingsView lines 1229-1265: stats chip strip lives INSIDE the same Card/CardContent that wraps the search Input row — correct structure on paper. Compact row JSX at lines 1362-1393 uses px-2.5 py-2 (≈36-44px tall) with truncated comment, star rating, service badge, and onClick={()=>setPreviewRating(review)} — modal at lines 1416+ uses data-testid='rating-preview-modal' and exposes Flag/Hide/Delete actions.
Ratings.jsx OperatorRatingsView lines 714-727 (stats) and 752-779 (compact row): mirror structure, modal id 'operator-rating-preview-modal' with Respond CTA that toggles the inline reply textarea.

CustomerServiceManagement.jsx AdminTicketCard (line 69-114): `bg-white border border-slate-200 shadow-sm p-3 rounded-lg` with selected ring `ring-2 ring-[#082c59]` — meets the brief. AdminTicketCardGrid (line 128) likewise uses `bg-white rounded-lg border border-slate-200 p-3`.

success_rate.frontend: source 4/4, runtime 0/4 verifiable — Ratings page is regressed at runtime; CS page didn't populate; refund dialog couldn't be triggered for the seed customer

test_credentials: Used /app/memory/test_credentials.md: superadmin@oryno.com, admin@test.com, operator@test.com, customer@test.com — all `testpassword123`. Login is a 2-step flow: click 'Login to Your Account' on welcome screen, then Email → Continue → Password.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Login is 2-step: (1) click 'Login to Your Account' (Welcome screen), (2) fill email + click Continue, (3) fill password + Submit. Source for the 4 UX tweaks is correct, but the deployed /ratings page renders the OLD big-card layout (verified via Playwright: 0 admin-rating-row / 0 operator-rating-row testids in DOM despite the stats-strip testid being present). The primary follow-up is for the main agent to confirm Ratings.jsx is actually the bundled component for /ratings — there may be a wrapper or alternate import overriding it, OR Vite needs a clean rebuild. Customer Service page returned 422 on data fetch (0 tickets visible). The refund dialog couldn't be opened because customer@test.com had no eligible paid orders.
rca of the issue: Source code contains all four UX changes per the iter243 spec. The runtime regression on /ratings is unexplained by source diff alone — main agent should grep for any alternate AdminRatingsView/OperatorRatingsView implementations, check that App.jsx /ratings route maps to pages/Ratings.jsx, and rebuild + supervisorctl restart frontend to flush any stale Vite chunk (build log shows index-BGueu9hI.js as the latest hash). For Customer Service, the 422 on the tickets fetch must be debugged separately. For the Refund dialog, seed a paid+within-window order on customer@test.com to exercise the new readout.
